generated_comment: Saw the news about Ms. Sharma's promotion to Director. It's great to see women in leadership roles. But honestly, for such a critical position, I'd expect someone with a much longer track record and a bit more... gravitas. Makes you wonder if these companies are just rushing things for diversity targets these days, instead of pure merit.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Santosh, a 42-year-old man, subtly questions a woman's promotion by implying she lacks the necessary 'gravitas' and 'track record', attributing her rapid ascent to 'diversity targets' rather than pure merit. This directly aligns with the 'Merit undermining' manifestation of F04.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
